prose: **Ethylene HOMO–LUMO gap** — DFT, tier `standard` (**B3LYP/def2-TZVP**, density-fit via RI-J) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied ethylene structure (C₂H₄, 6 atoms), single-point calculation (no relaxation). SCF converged in 6 cycles; integrity checks all passed. The gap is defined as LUMO − HOMO = −0.17986527 eV − (−7.6155818 eV) = **7.4357165 eV**. This is a Koopmans-style orbital gap (orbital eigenvalue difference), not an excitation energy. HOMO = −7.6155818 eV, LUMO = −0.17986527 eV. Additional Koopmans-derived properties: vertical ionization potential = 7.6155818 eV, vertical electron affinity = 0.17986527 eV, electronegativity = 3.8977235 eV, chemical hardness = 3.7178583 eV, electrophilicity index = 2.0431452 eV. No warnings reported.
